Operation 'registerApplication' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. The current description is too brief and lacks critical details about what inputs are required (application name, redirect URI), what outputs are returned (client_id, client_secret), and important security context about credential storage. Enhanced description provides AI agents with complete information about the registration workflow and credential management.

data.current_description: Register a new application to obtain client credentials for API access.
data.description_suggestion: Register a new OAuth2 application to obtain client credentials (client_id and client_secret) for programmatic API access. Requires providing application name and redirect URI. Returns the generated credentials needed for subsequent token requests. These credentials should be securely stored and used only with the /auth_tokens/token/ endpoint.
data.reasoning: The current description is too brief and lacks critical details about what inputs are required (application name, redirect URI), what outputs are returned (client_id, client_secret), and important security context about credential storage. Enhanced description provides AI agents with complete information about the registration workflow and credential management.

message: Operation 'getAccessToken' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_context. Current description lacks specificity about required inputs (client_id, client_secret), token format and usage (Bearer token in Authorization header), and token lifecycle (expiration, refresh). Enhanced description provides AI agents with complete workflow context including how to use the returned token and when re-authentication may be needed.

data.current_description: Exchange client credentials for an API access token.
data.description_suggestion: Exchange OAuth2 client credentials for a Bearer access token. Requires client_id and client_secret obtained from application registration. Returns an access token with an expiration time (typically 1 hour). The returned token should be included in the Authorization header as 'Bearer <token>' for authenticated API requests. Token refresh may be required after expiration.
data.reasoning: Current description lacks specificity about required inputs (client_id, client_secret), token format and usage (Bearer token in Authorization header), and token lifecycle (expiration, refresh). Enhanced description provides AI agents with complete workflow context including how to use the returned token and when re-authentication may be needed.

message: Operation 'getRateLimit' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_context. Current description doesn't explain what information is returned or how rate limits differ based on authentication status. Enhanced description clarifies the response content (quota, remaining requests, reset time), the operation's purpose (monitoring usage), and important context about authentication-dependent rate limits to help AI agents understand when and why to call this endpoint.

data.current_description: Check the current rate limit status for your API key.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieve the current rate limit status for the authenticated API key or session. Returns information about request quota, remaining requests, and reset time. Useful for monitoring API usage and determining if rate limits have been exceeded. Rate limits vary based on authentication status: unauthenticated requests have lower limits than authenticated requests with a valid Bearer token.
data.reasoning: Current description doesn't explain what information is returned or how rate limits differ based on authentication status. Enhanced description clarifies the response content (quota, remaining requests, reset time), the operation's purpose (monitoring usage), and important context about authentication-dependent rate limits to help AI agents understand when and why to call this endpoint.

data.current_description: Register a new application to obtain client credentials for API access.
data.description_suggestion: Register a new OAuth2 application to obtain client credentials (client_id and client_secret) for programmatic API access. Requires providing application name and redirect URI. Returns the generated credentials needed for subsequent token requests. These credentials should be securely stored and used only with the /auth_tokens/token/ endpoint.
data.reasoning: The current description is too brief and lacks critical details about what inputs are required (application name, redirect URI), what outputs are returned (client_id, client_secret), and important security context about credential storage. Enhanced description provides AI agents with complete information about the registration workflow and credential management.

message: Operation 'getAccessToken' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_context. Current description lacks specificity about required inputs (client_id, client_secret), token format and usage (Bearer token in Authorization header), and token lifecycle (expiration, refresh). Enhanced description provides AI agents with complete workflow context including how to use the returned token and when re-authentication may be needed.

data.current_description: Exchange client credentials for an API access token.
data.description_suggestion: Exchange OAuth2 client credentials for a Bearer access token. Requires client_id and client_secret obtained from application registration. Returns an access token with an expiration time (typically 1 hour). The returned token should be included in the Authorization header as 'Bearer <token>' for authenticated API requests. Token refresh may be required after expiration.
data.reasoning: Current description lacks specificity about required inputs (client_id, client_secret), token format and usage (Bearer token in Authorization header), and token lifecycle (expiration, refresh). Enhanced description provides AI agents with complete workflow context including how to use the returned token and when re-authentication may be needed.

message: Operation 'getRateLimit' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_context. Current description doesn't explain what information is returned or how rate limits differ based on authentication status. Enhanced description clarifies the response content (quota, remaining requests, reset time), the operation's purpose (monitoring usage), and important context about authentication-dependent rate limits to help AI agents understand when and why to call this endpoint.

data.current_description: Check the current rate limit status for your API key.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieve the current rate limit status for the authenticated API key or session. Returns information about request quota, remaining requests, and reset time. Useful for monitoring API usage and determining if rate limits have been exceeded. Rate limits vary based on authentication status: unauthenticated requests have lower limits than authenticated requests with a valid Bearer token.
data.reasoning: Current description doesn't explain what information is returned or how rate limits differ based on authentication status. Enhanced description clarifies the response content (quota, remaining requests, reset time), the operation's purpose (monitoring usage), and important context about authentication-dependent rate limits to help AI agents understand when and why to call this endpoint.
